Card.title: Card
Card.description: Represents a card in the card game.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Card entity.

ability.description: Description of the card's special ability or effect.

flavorText.description: Descriptive flavor text of the card.

attack.description: The attack power of the card.

defense.description: The defense or health points of the card.

manaCost.description: The mana cost required to play the card.

cardType.description: The type of the card (e.g., monster, spell, trap).

cardRarity.description: The rarity of the card (e.g., common, uncommon, rare).

visualTheme.description: The visual theme of the card (e.g., fantasy, sci-fi).

imagePlaceholder.description: Placeholder or URL for the card's image.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The email address of the user.

eloRating.description: The ELO rating of the user for matchmaking.

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents an online match between two players.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

startTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the match started.

endTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the match ended.

winnerId.description: Reference to User. The id of the winner of the match. Null if the match is a draw or still in progress. (Relationship: User 1:N Match)

matchData.description: The data that represents the match, stored as a JSON string.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership guarantees only the authenticated user can access their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores user-created card data. Path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access cards they've created.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: cardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the card.

definition.description: Stores match data for online games. Includes player IDs, start/end times, and winner.  The match data itself (`matchData`) is stored as a JSON string to allow flexibility in representing the game state.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the match.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the CardCrafter application, emphasizing secure online matches and user-generated card content.  Authorization Independence is achieved through a path-based ownership model for user data and denormalization of match data. The segregation of card data by ownership (user-created vs. system cards) ensures consistent security rules. The online matches are structured for real-time data and efficient querying.

Specifically:

*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership guarantees only the authenticated user can access their profile.
*   `/users/{userId}/cards/{cardId}`:  Enables users to manage their custom card collection. Path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access cards they've created. This structure allows secure listing of a user's cards without needing to filter based on user ID in the rules.
*   `/matches/{matchId}`: Stores match data, including player IDs, start/end times, and winner. Match data includes `player1Id` and `player2Id`, which can be used to implement security rules that ensure only the involved players can access or modify the match data.  The match data itself (`matchData`) is stored as a JSON string to allow flexibility in representing the game state. Because access control is based on `matchId` this supports secure `list` operations, as only those with knowledge of the `matchId` can attempt to access match data.

This structure facilitates the addition of online matches as requested, while maintaining the core design principles of security and scalability.
